title: Mega Aerodactyl Battle Guide
summary: A 150 Speed [rock]/[flying] sweeper whose [tough-claws] ability turns every contact move into a 1.3x boosted finisher.
strengths: 150 Speed outruns virtually everything in the format
[tough-claws] boosts every contact move by 30 percent
85/95 bulk is actually survivable compared to the base form
weaknesses: Still vulnerable to strong special attackers
Ice, Electric, and [rock] weaknesses persist from the type combo
Turn one before mega evolving still runs the base ability

recommendedMoves.reason: High base power [rock] STAB for breaking walls
Ground coverage for Steel and Electric switch-ins
Contact coverage for Dragon and Ground targets

recommendedItems.reason: Required mega stone for the Aerodactyl mega form

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Aerodactyl is a 150 Speed physical sweeper whose role is to dominate the Speed tier absolutely. The base 135 Attack is only average for a top tier sweeper, but the [tough-claws] ability adds a 30 percent multiplier to every contact move. [aerial-ace], [ice-fang], crunch, and return all scale from the ability boost, which makes Mega Aerodactyl hit much harder than the raw stats suggest.

## Strengths
150 Speed is one of the highest tiers. [tough-claws] is the damage scaler that turns moderate-power moves into real threats, and the bulk bump from 65 to 85 on Defense means Mega Aerodactyl survives incidental chip damage that would have ended the base form. The flexible coverage combined with the speed advantage means Mega Aerodactyl can switch between roles mid-match.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Special attackers are still the biggest problem — thunderbolt and ice-beam from the faster special attackers in the format do enough damage to matter even with the improved bulk. The [rock]/[flying] type combination still carries multiple useful weaknesses, and stealth-[rock] chip remains a concern on the first entry. Turn one is still the base ability ([rock]-head or unnerve), so the first attack pre-mega can behave unexpectedly.

## Recommended Moveset
[stone-edge] is the locked [rock] STAB because it's still the best raw-damage finisher, even though it doesn't benefit from [tough-claws]. [aerial-ace] is the [tough-claws] [flying] STAB with locked accuracy. [earthquake] is the Ground coverage that handles Electric and Steel switch-ins, and [ice-fang] fills the last coverage slot for Dragon and Ground targets. crunch is an alternate pick if the team needs Ghost coverage instead.

## Item · Ability · Tera
The item is always [aerodactylite]. Post-mega, the ability is locked to [tough-claws]. The pre-mega turn should be used to pick a safe matchup, not to set up or attack. Tera [flying] erases the Ground weakness and boosts [aerial-ace] further, while Tera [rock] maxes [stone-edge] for a single big finisher against a known wall.

## Teammates & Synergy
A special wall (specially defensive Steel or Fairy) partners well because Mega Aerodactyl struggles against fast special attackers. A Ground partner covers Electric threats, and a rapid-spin or defog user keeps hazards from chipping the bulk. Slow pivots like volt-switch or u-turn create the safe entry turns Mega Aerodactyl needs, and a breaker on the back line picks up walls Mega Aerodactyl can't punch through quickly.

## Play Patterns
Use the pre-mega turn to enter on a forced switch, mega evolve, and pick the correct coverage for the incoming target — [aerial-ace] for Grass and Fighting, [stone-edge] for [flying] and Fire, [earthquake] for Steel and Electric, [ice-fang] for Ground and Dragon. Don't commit to [stone-edge] blindly, because its accuracy still loses matches. Save the Tera for the exact moment an Ice or Electric move is telegraphed, and use it defensively to preserve the sweep.
